casestudy.scenario: Contoso has recently licensed Dynamics 365 Customer Service and is setting up the system from scratch. The support centre has the following requirements: 1. All inbound emails to support@contoso.com must automatically create cases 2. Cases must be categorised using a standard product taxonomy 3. Agents should only be able to edit cases they personally own 4. Supervisors must be able to view and reassign all cases across the team 5. Cases must be resolved within 4 hours of creation, with a warning at 3 hours 6. Agents must confirm the customer was notified before resolving a case The admin team is configuring all of these requirements before going live.

questions.q: To ensure agents can only edit cases they personally own, what should the Write privilege on the Case entity be set to in the agent security role?
options: Organisation
Business Unit
User
None
questions.explanation: Setting Write to User level restricts agents to editing only the case records they own. They may still have Read at a higher level to see other cases, but Write at User level prevents modifying records they do not own.
wrongExplanations: Organisation-level Write allows editing any case in the system — too permissive.
Business Unit-level Write allows editing cases owned by anyone in the team — broader than required.
None would prevent editing any cases, including their own.

questions.q: The supervisor needs to view and reassign all cases across the team. Which out-of-box security role is most appropriate?
options: Customer Service Representative
CSR Manager
System Administrator
Basic User
questions.explanation: The CSR Manager role provides Business Unit-level read/write on Cases, including the ability to reassign cases, without granting the full unrestricted access of System Administrator.
wrongExplanations: Customer Service Representative has User-level write — supervisors cannot reassign other agents cases with this role.
System Administrator has full unrestricted system access — inappropriate for a supervisory agent role.
Basic User has insufficient permissions for case management.

questions.q: The organisation wants agents to be able to read, create and update cases but not delete them. Which set of privileges should the Case entity have on the agent security role?
options: Read, Write, Delete at Business Unit level
Read, Write, Create at the appropriate access level — no Delete privilege
Read, Write, Create, Delete at User level only
Full CRUD — agents need Delete to resolve and close cases
questions.explanation: To prevent deletion while allowing normal case management, the role should include Read, Write, and Create — but not Delete. Closing or resolving a case is done via a status change (Write), not by deleting the record. Removing the Delete privilege from the agent role enforces this boundary.

wrongExplanations.0: Including Delete at Business Unit level would still allow agents to delete cases — just limited to their own BU. The requirement is no deletion at all.
wrongExplanations.2: Including Delete even at User level still permits deletion — the requirement is to exclude Delete entirely from the role.
wrongExplanations.3: Resolving a case changes its status — it does not require the Delete privilege. Adding Delete would be against the stated requirement.

questions.q: A new role is created but the user reports they cannot see the Customer Service workspace app in their apps list, even though all entity privileges are correctly set. What is missing?
options: The user has not been added to the correct queue
App Module — Read at Global is not included in the custom role
The System Administrator role is required to see any apps
The user must be in the same business unit as the app owner
questions.explanation: App Module — Read at Global controls whether a model-driven app appears in the user's app launcher. This is a non-obvious privilege that is separate from all entity-level privileges. Without it, the app will not appear even if all other access is correctly configured.

wrongExplanations.0: Queue membership is for work routing — it has no bearing on app visibility in the launcher.
wrongExplanations.2: System Administrator is not required to see or use apps — the App Module Read privilege on the custom role is what controls this.
wrongExplanations.3: Business unit alignment affects data access scope, not app launcher visibility.

questions.q: Contoso wants to ensure that when multiple security roles are assigned to an agent, the agent gets the broadest possible access across all roles — not the most restrictive. Is this how D365 Customer Service works?
options: No — D365 applies the most restrictive of the assigned roles to prevent privilege escalation
Yes — D365 uses least privilege across roles, requiring all roles to grant access before it is allowed
Yes — D365 uses the most permissive combination: if any role grants a privilege, the user has it
It depends on the role type — predefined roles use most permissive, custom roles use most restrictive
questions.explanation: Dynamics 365 / Dataverse applies the most permissive combination when multiple security roles are assigned to a user. If any one of the assigned roles grants a particular privilege, the user has that privilege — regardless of whether another role restricts it. This is a fundamental principle of the Dataverse security model and means administrators must be careful not to inadvertently grant excess access through role combinations.

wrongExplanations.0: D365 does NOT apply the most restrictive role — it takes the most permissive across all assigned roles.
wrongExplanations.1: Least privilege (requiring all roles to agree) is not how Dataverse works — any single role granting access is sufficient.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no difference in behaviour between predefined and custom roles — both follow the most permissive combination rule.

casestudy.title: Security Role Design Challenge
casestudy.scenario: Northwind Traders is rolling out Dynamics 365 Customer Service to three types of users: Tier 1 Agents: Handle inbound cases, can read all cases but only edit their own, cannot delete cases Tier 2 Specialists: Handle escalated cases, can edit any case in their business unit, can delete cases within their BU Supervisors: Can view, edit, and reassign any case organisation-wide, can access all queues and run reports The admin must design security roles that match these requirements precisely without over-permissioning any group. Additionally, some cases contain sensitive financial data in a custom field called Contract Value. Only Tier 2 Specialists and Supervisors should see this field.

questions.q: For Tier 1 Agents — Read all cases, Write only their own, no Delete. Which privilege configuration is correct?
options: Read: Organisation, Write: Organisation, Delete: None
Read: Organisation, Write: User, Delete: None
Read: Business Unit, Write: User, Delete: User
Read: User, Write: User, Delete: None
questions.explanation: Read: Organisation lets Tier 1 see all cases. Write: User restricts editing to their own cases. Delete: None prevents any case deletion.
wrongExplanations: Read: Org, Write: Org would allow editing all cases — Tier 1 agents should only edit their own.
Read: Business Unit would restrict visibility to their BU — they need Organisation-level Read.
Read: User would prevent them from seeing other agents cases — the requirement is to read all cases.

questions.q: For Tier 2 Specialists — edit and delete any case in their Business Unit. Which privilege configuration is correct?
options: Read: Organisation, Write: Organisation, Delete: Organisation
Read: Business Unit, Write: Business Unit, Delete: Business Unit
Read: Organisation, Write: Business Unit, Delete: Business Unit
Read: User, Write: Business Unit, Delete: Business Unit
questions.explanation: Read: Organisation lets Tier 2 see all cases to support escalation from any team. Write and Delete at Business Unit level restricts modifications to cases within their own BU.
wrongExplanations: Write and Delete at Organisation would allow modifying any case in the system — too permissive for Tier 2.
Read at Business Unit would restrict visibility to their BU only — Tier 2 specialists handling escalations may need to see cases from other teams.
Read at User only shows their own cases — Tier 2 needs broader read access.

questions.q: Supervisors need organisation-wide access to cases. Rather than building a role from scratch, which out-of-box role is the closest starting point?
options: Customer Service Representative
CSR Manager
System Administrator
Knowledge Manager
questions.explanation: The CSR Manager role provides Business Unit-level supervisory access as a starting point and can be cloned and elevated to Organisation-level for the Supervisor role requirement.
wrongExplanations: Customer Service Representative is an agent role — it does not have supervisory access levels.
System Administrator has unrestricted full access including system customisation — far too broad for a supervisor role.
Knowledge Manager is focused on knowledge article management — not case supervision.

questions.q: The Contract Value custom field must be hidden from Tier 1 Agents but visible to Tier 2 and Supervisors. Which feature restricts access to a specific field?
options: Form customisation — remove the field from Tier 1 case form
Field Security Profile — restrict Read access on the Contract Value field
Business Rule — hide the field based on the user security role
Column-level permissions in the Case View
questions.explanation: Field Security Profiles control access to individual fields at the data level. By enabling field security on Contract Value and creating a profile that grants Read access to Tier 2 and Supervisors only, Tier 1 agents cannot see the field value even via API.
wrongExplanations: Removing the field from a form hides it in the UI but data is still accessible via other forms, views, or the API — not a true security boundary.
Business Rules hide fields on specific forms in the browser — they do not restrict data access via API or other forms.
Column-level permissions in views are a UI display setting — not a security control that enforces data-level access.

questions.q: A Tier 1 agent is accidentally assigned both the Tier 1 role (Write: User on Case) and the Tier 2 role (Write: Business Unit on Case). What is their effective Write access on Case?
options: User — the most restrictive role takes precedence
Business Unit — the most permissive role wins (union principle)
No access — conflicting roles cancel each other
Organisation — roles are averaged upward
questions.explanation: Dynamics 365 uses a union (most permissive) model when combining security roles. Business Unit is broader than User, so the agent effectively has Business Unit-level Write — the Tier 2 role overrides the restriction of the Tier 1 role for that privilege.
wrongExplanations: Dynamics 365 never applies the most restrictive role — it always takes the most permissive level across all assigned roles.
Roles never cancel — they combine via union.
There is no averaging mechanism — the highest level from any role applies.

casestudy.title: Northwind Contact Centre — User Setup & Role Design

scenario.title: Northwind Contact Centre — User Onboarding and Role Configuration Challenge
scenario.background:
Northwind Contact Centre is deploying Dynamics 365 Customer Service for a 120-person team. The team has three tiers: Tier 1 Agents (80 reps), Tier 2 Specialists (30 reps), and 10 Supervisors. The IT administrator has created all user accounts in Microsoft 365 and assigned licences. The organisation also has two system integrations using API-based connectors — one for CRM-to-ERP data sync and one for a reporting tool. The administrator has received the following requirements:

R1. All agents and supervisors must be able to access the Customer Service app.
R2. Supervisors must be able to monitor, assign, and transfer conversations.
R3. Agents must work with Case records.
R4. The CRM-to-ERP connector must authenticate against Dataverse without a UI login.
R5. A lead administrator needs to configure the environment including managing user roles — but must also avoid consuming a user licence unnecessarily.
R6. Agents should have their skills, queues, and capacity profiles configured efficiently at scale.
requirements: R1: All agents and supervisors must be able to access the Customer Service app.
R2: Supervisors must be able to monitor, assign, and transfer conversations.
R3: Agents must work with Case records.
R4: The CRM-to-ERP connector must authenticate against Dataverse without a UI login.
R5: Lead administrator needs to configure the environment and manage user roles — no unnecessary licence consumption.
R6: Agents should have skills, queues, and capacity profiles configured efficiently at scale.

questions.q: For R1 and R2 — which combination of roles should the 10 supervisors receive?
options: Basic User + Omnichannel supervisor + Customer Service Representative
Basic User + Omnichannel supervisor + App Profile User
Basic User + Omnichannel administrator + Omnichannel supervisor
Omnichannel supervisor + App Profile User only
questions.explanation: Supervisors need Basic User (required by all users), Omnichannel supervisor (for monitoring, assigning, and transferring conversations), and App Profile User (required by users of Customer Service and Contact Center). Customer Service Representative is for working with the Case entity — supervisors typically don't need this. Omnichannel administrator would give them unnecessary configuration access.

wrongExplanations.0: Customer Service Representative is for agents working with Case records — not the correct addition for supervisors whose primary function is oversight.
wrongExplanations.2: Omnichannel administrator should not be assigned to supervisors — it gives configuration access they don't need and requires System Administrator for full effect.
wrongExplanations.3: Omnichannel supervisor alone and App Profile User alone are insufficient — Basic User is required by all users as the baseline role.

questions.q: For R3 — which additional role must Tier 1 Agents receive beyond Basic User, Omnichannel agent, and App Profile User?
options: Omnichannel supervisor — to access the case queue
Customer Service Representative — required to work with the Case entity
System Administrator — required for Case read access
No additional role is needed — Omnichannel agent already covers Case access
questions.explanation: The Customer Service Representative role is specifically required to work with the Case entity in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. The Omnichannel agent role alone does not grant Case entity access. This role must be added in addition to the baseline roles.

wrongExplanations.0: Omnichannel supervisor gives supervisory capabilities — not Case entity access for agents.
wrongExplanations.2: System Administrator is an admin role — agents should never receive this for case access.
wrongExplanations.3: Omnichannel agent alone does not cover Case entity access — Customer Service Representative is explicitly required for Case.

questions.q: For R4 — the CRM-to-ERP connector needs to authenticate against Dataverse. What type of user account should be created, and what is the correct access mode?
options: A regular Read-Write user with a dedicated D365 licence assigned permanently
A non-interactive user account — licence assigned during setup, then removed after setting the Non-interactive access mode
An administrative user account — no licence required and provides system-level API access
A stub user — automatically created when the connector first authenticates
questions.explanation: A non-interactive user account is the correct choice for API connectors that need to authenticate without UI sign-in. The process: create the account in M365, assign a licence, set the access mode to Non-interactive in the D365 user form, then remove the licence from M365. Up to 7 non-interactive users are allowed per environment.

wrongExplanations.0: A permanent Read-Write user is designed for human sign-in — it consumes a licence unnecessarily and is not the intended pattern for API connectors.
wrongExplanations.2: Administrative access mode has no access to customer data or app functionality — it cannot be used for CRM data sync operations.
wrongExplanations.3: Stub users are placeholders created by data imports — they cannot sign in, cannot be enabled, and cannot be used for integration authentication.

questions.q: For R5 — the lead administrator needs to configure the environment and manage user roles. Which role combination achieves this without consuming a user licence unnecessarily?
options: Omnichannel administrator + Basic User — no licence required for admin roles
Omnichannel administrator + System Administrator — set access mode to Administrative after setup, then remove the licence
System Administrator only — licence is required permanently for this role
Omnichannel administrator + System Administrator — no licence is ever needed for admin users
questions.explanation: To manage user roles and privileges, both Omnichannel administrator and System Administrator roles are required. For administrative users who don't access customer data, you set the access mode to Administrative after setup and then remove the licence. Microsoft documents that Global admins, Power Platform admins, and D365 admins don't need a licence to be enabled — but the licence must be removed after setting the Administrative access mode.

wrongExplanations.0: Omnichannel administrator + Basic User is insufficient — System Administrator is required to manage user roles and privileges.
wrongExplanations.2: A licence is not permanently required — the administrative access mode allows licence removal after setup.
wrongExplanations.3: A licence is needed during initial setup — it can only be removed after setting the Administrative access mode.

questions.q: For R6 — configuring skills, queues, and capacity profiles for 80 agents individually would take days. What is the most efficient approach?
options: Use the Power Platform admin center bulk user import feature to set skills in a CSV upload
Use the Enhanced user management in Copilot Service admin center — select multiple users and use Update user attributes to bulk-update skills, queues, and capacity profiles
Use Dataverse API bulk calls — send up to 500 change requests every 15 minutes for optimal throughput
Create a shared team in D365 and assign the team a skill set — all team members inherit the skills
questions.explanation: The Enhanced user management experience in Copilot Service admin center is designed exactly for this scenario. Administrators can select multiple users and use Update user attributes to add skills (with proficiency), update queue assignments, and assign capacity profiles — all in bulk. This is the recommended UI approach. The Dataverse API (option 2) is valid for very large scale but is a developer/admin API approach, not the standard admin UI workflow.

wrongExplanations.0: The Power Platform admin center does not have a CSV skill import feature for user attributes.
wrongExplanations.2: The API approach is valid at scale but is a technical/developer method — Enhanced user management is the intended admin UI solution for this scenario.
wrongExplanations.3: Skills in D365 Customer Service are configured on the Bookable Resource record for each individual user — they are not inherited through team membership.
